title: PTEN Genetic Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
or your child may need a test if you have symptoms of PHTS, which include:
Having many hamartomas, especially in the mouth and intestines, or on the breasts or skin. 
A larger than normal head size (macrocephaly). 
Developmental delays , learning disabilities , and/or autism . 
Dark freckles on the penis. 
Thyroid , kidney , or colorectal cancer . 
Uterine or breast cancer (in females only). 
If you're pregnant and have a family history of PTEN gene changes , you may consider having the test as part of your prenatal testing . The test can show whether your baby could be born with PHTS.
What happens during a PTEN genetic test? 
If you're having a PTEN genetic test because you have cancer, the type of sample used will depend on your cancer:
For solid tumors, the test often uses a sample of tissue from the tumor. The tissue sample may be taken during surgery to remove the tumor. If you're not having surgery, you may have a biopsy of the tumor to remove a small amount of tissue. Health care professionals can do this by performing:
A fine needle aspiration biopsy. This procedure uses a very thin needle to remove a sample of cells from the tumor. 
A core needle biopsy. This procedure uses a larger needle to remove a sample. 
For blood or bone marrow cancer, a bone marrow test is often used. Marrow is soft, spongy tissue found inside of bones. Most bone marrow samples are taken from t
